title: Alexia Zuberer

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [ski mountaineering](/wiki/Ski_mountaineering "Ski mountaineering") | | | | Representing  [France](/wiki/France "France") | | | | [European Championships](/wiki/European_Championships_of_Ski_Mountaineering "European Championships of Ski Mountaineering") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2001 France](/wiki/2001_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2001 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") | Team | |

(born 1972[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is a [Swiss](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland")-[French](/wiki/France "France") [ski mountaineer](/wiki/Ski_mountaineer "Ski mountaineer"), ski instructor and [mountain guide](/wiki/Mountain_guide "Mountain guide"), and took also part in several [Himalayan](/wiki/Himalayas "Himalayas") expeditions.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[*Coupe d'Europe 2001 de Ski-alpinisme*](http://www.ffme.fr/ski-alpinisme/actualite/2001/02gastlosen.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20071202051524/http://ffme.fr/ski-alpinisme/actualite/2001/02gastlosen.htm) 2007-12-02 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

[*Pierre Gignoux et Stéphane Brosse Champions d'Europe!*](http://www.ffme.fr/ski-alpinisme/actualite/2001/01contamine.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20071210104952/http://ffme.fr/ski-alpinisme/actualite/2001/01contamine.htm) 2007-12-10 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

[*Albo d'oro - Roll of Honour*](http://www.cimadastaskialp.it/ita/Albo.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20110722025935/http://www.cimadastaskialp.it/ita/Albo.htm) 2011-07-22 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

[*Trophée des Gastlosen (FR), 17 février*](http://alpen.sac-cas.ch/html_f/archiv/2002/200206/af_2002_06_07.pdf) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20110707002942/http://alpen.sac-cas.ch/html_f/archiv/2002/200206/af_2002_06_07.pdf) 2011-07-07 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), 2002, p. 3.
[Makalu 2010 expedition sum up published by one of the Mountaineers, Philippe Bourgine](https://www.dailymotion.com/video/xng6rr_expedition-makalu-2010-en-resume-4-20-en-images-et-en-musique_sport)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Alexia Zuberer](/w/index.php?title=Alexia_Zuberer&redirect=no "Alexia Zuberer"))

Alexia Zuberer

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | 1972 (age 53–54) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | Skiing |
|  | |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [ski mountaineering](/wiki/Ski_mountaineering "Ski mountaineering") | | | | Representing  [France](/wiki/France "France") | | | | [European Championships](/wiki/European_Championships_of_Ski_Mountaineering "European Championships of Ski Mountaineering") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2001 France](/wiki/2001_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2001 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") | Team | | |

**Aléxia Zuberer** (born 1972[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is a [Swiss](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland")-[French](/wiki/France "France") [ski mountaineer](/wiki/Ski_mountaineer "Ski mountaineer"), ski instructor and [mountain guide](/wiki/Mountain_guide "Mountain guide"), and took also part in several [Himalayan](/wiki/Himalayas "Himalayas") expeditions.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Selected results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al%C3%A9xia_Zuberer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Selected results")]

* 1998:
  + 1st, [Tour du Rutor](/w/index.php?title=Tour_du_Rutor&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tour du Rutor (page does not exist)") (together with [Claudine Trécourt](/wiki/Claudine_Tr%C3%A9court "Claudine Trécourt"))[[3]](#cite_note-3)
  + 2nd, [Patrouille des Glaciers](/wiki/Patrouille_des_Glaciers "Patrouille des Glaciers") together with Claudine Trécourt and [Jana Heczková](/wiki/Jana_Heczkov%C3%A1 "Jana Heczková"))
* 2001:
  + 1st, [Trophée des Gastlosen](/wiki/Troph%C3%A9e_des_Gastlosen "Trophée des Gastlosen") (European Cup, together with [Gloriana Pellissier](/wiki/Gloriana_Pellissier "Gloriana Pellissier"))[[4]](#cite_note-4)
  + 1st, [Trofeo Mezzalama](/wiki/Trofeo_Mezzalama "Trofeo Mezzalama") (together with Gloriana Pellissier and [Arianna Follis](/wiki/Arianna_Follis "Arianna Follis"))
  + 3rd, [European Championship](/wiki/2001_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2001 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") team race (together with Gloriana Pellissier)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* 2002:
  + 1st, [Transacavallo](/w/index.php?title=Transacavallo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Transacavallo (page does not exist)") (together with Gloriana Pellissier)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
  + 1st, Dolomiti Cup team (together with Gloriana Pellissier)[[7]](#cite_note-Dolomiti_Cup-7)
  + 2nd, Trophée des Gastlosen (together with Gloriana Pellissier)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### Pierra Menta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al%C3%A9xia_Zuberer&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pierra Menta")]

Main article: [Pierra Menta](/wiki/Pierra_Menta "Pierra Menta")

* 1995: 2nd, together with Claudine Trécourt
* 1997: 1st, together with Claudine Trécourt
* 1998: 1st, together with Claudine Trécourt
* 1999: 3rd, together with [Véronique Lathuraz](/wiki/V%C3%A9ronique_Lathuraz "Véronique Lathuraz")
* 2000: 1st, together with [Gloriana Pellissier](/wiki/Gloriana_Pellissier "Gloriana Pellissier")
* 2001: 1st, together with Gloriana Pellissier
* 2005, 8th, together with [Sylvie Ferragu](/w/index.php?title=Sylvie_Ferragu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sylvie Ferragu (page does not exist)")

## Expeditions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al%C3%A9xia_Zuberer&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Expeditions")]

* 1996 : Cho Oyu - Tibet (8,201 metres (26,906 ft))
* 1997 : Shishapangma - Tibet (8,013 metres (26,289 ft))
* 2002 : Ama Dablam - Nepal (6,857 metres (22,497 ft))
* 2003 : Everest - Nepal (8,850 metres (29,040 ft))
* 2007 : Everest - Tibet (8,850 metres (29,040 ft), without oxygen up to 8,500 metres (27,900 ft))
* 2008 : Mustagh Ata - China (7,545 metres (24,754 ft))
* 2010 : Makalu-Nepal (8,470 metres (27,790 ft)) with the [Mountains-6](/wiki/MountainsMap "MountainsMap") Makalu 2010 expedition, first Swiss woman to reach the summit.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* 2011 : Kangchenjunga India/Nepal (8,586 metres (28,169 ft))

## Publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al%C3%A9xia_Zuberer&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Publications")]

* *Pierra Menta - Traces d'une Légende*, 2005
* *Double ascension à l'Everest*, Nevicata Editions, 2008. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-2-9600255-5-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-2-9600255-5-2 "Special:BookSources/978-2-9600255-5-2").
